on

President Bola Tinubu has celebrated Rena Wakama for winning the best coach in the women’s basketball event at the 2024 Olympic Games.

Wakama, who is the D’Tigress head coach, led the Nigerian side to a historic quarter final outing at the Games before bowing to the US.

D’Tigress made history as the first African team to reach the quarter finals in the history of the Olympics.

Reacting to the development on Monday, a statement issued by the Special Adviser on Media and Publicity to the President, Ajuri Ngelale, said, “The recognition of Ms. Wakama, 32, by the International Basketball Federation, FIBA, further accents her well established leadership qualities and dedication to excellence.

“The President also commends the team, and all those who played a part in the unprecedented feat at the Olympics, enjoining them not to relent or become crestfallen but to remain resilient and determined for greatness.

“The President further thanks the Nigerian contingent and wished them success in their respective endeavours.”
